core_mechanism: a noise-controlled collective-synchronization phase transition in a population of coupled stochastic (limit-cycle) oscillators, where a single order parameter emerges above a critical value of the per-oscillator coherence and exhibits distinct critical scaling on either side of the threshold
brief_reason: The abstract reports a phase transition to synchronization among stochastic oscillators that depends only on single-oscillator coherence, with different scaling above and below the transition, which is exactly a coupled-noisy-oscillator synchronization transition governed by individual-unit coherence.
